title: Businesses get free OCC buzz on 15 March
description: GEORGE NEWS - The Outeniqua Chair Challenge...
bodyText:
GEORGE NEWS - The Outeniqua Chair Challenge Event Organiser, Alewijn Dippenaar of the Disabled Road Race Foundation, is inviting local business owners in George to be an integral part of the 21st Outeniqua Chair Challenge on Saturday 15 March.

Dippenaar encourages Georgians to join this unique event in celebration of the determination and resilience of athletes with disabilities. “We aim to gather 50+ businesses to participate in this special day.”


> And the best part? It’s free!


How to get involved• Pitch your gazebo along the route to showcase your business and cheer on the athletes.• Encourage your staff to participate and support the athletes.• Demonstrate your appreciation and passion for these incredible athletes.No cost involvedThere's no cost involved - just enthusiasm and commitment to making a difference!

To secure your spot along the route, contact Etienne Joubert from the Garden Route Injury Centre:Email: [email protected]Phone:  082 353 4251Great supportDippenaar extended the foundation’s heartfelt gratitude to each business that joins them in celebrating this extraordinary event. “Your support will make a tangible difference in the lives of these inspiring athletes. Let's come together to make the 21st Outeniqua Chair Challenge an unforgettable experience!”

The OCC was almost cancelled this year due to a R300 000 shortfall, but the Garden Route Mall and other sponsors saved the day.

Alewijn Dippenaar can be reached at [email protected] or on 082 457 5675.

‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
